clarity.readOnly.label: Read-only
clarity.readOnly.description: This Dashboard surface explains or reports state; changes are made through existing config or runtime flows.
clarity.configDriven.label: Config-driven
clarity.configDriven.description: Create or change this concept in gateway config or existing management flows; this release only clarifies the surface.
clarity.preview.label: Preview
clarity.preview.description: The surface is available for OSS inspection, but the feature boundary is intentionally narrow.
clarity.ossFixedRoles.label: OSS fixed roles
clarity.ossFixedRoles.description: Open-source roles are fixed to Admin, Operator, and Viewer. Custom role definitions are not added in this release.
clarity.runtimeSupported.label: Runtime-supported
clarity.runtimeSupported.description: The gateway runtime already enforces or records this concept; this page makes that boundary easier to see.
clarity.requiresConfig.label: Requires config
clarity.requiresConfig.description: Nothing appears until the related providers, namespaces, policies, or samples are configured.
concepts.workspace.title: Workspace is the active operating scope
concepts.workspace.description: A workspace scopes Dashboard data, RBAC membership, audit evidence, API keys, budgets, nodes, and logs. OSS bootstraps a default workspace and lets you switch existing workspaces; this release does not add workspace creation.
concepts.policyNamespace.title: Policy Namespace is a routing policy label
concepts.policyNamespace.description: A Policy Namespace groups config-driven gateway rules such as key permissions, budgets, routing limits, MCP allow-lists, shadow filters, and logs. It is not a workspace, tenant, team, or folder, and this release does not add namespace CRUD.
concepts.apiKeys.title: Gateway API Keys are client access scopes
concepts.apiKeys.description: Gateway API Keys identify calling applications and intersect Team, Policy Namespace, endpoint, model, node, budget, and rate-limit policy. The full key is shown once; stored views stay masked.
concepts.semanticControls.title: Semantic Controls are governed metadata controls
concepts.semanticControls.description: Semantic cache, prompt registry metadata, context evidence, intent hints, and guardrail findings help explain routing without storing prompts or responses by default.
concepts.trafficExperiments.title: Traffic Experiments report configured split traffic
concepts.trafficExperiments.description: This page reads A/B split metadata from routing configuration and compares latency, cost, success, and volume. It does not create experiments or change traffic weights.
concepts.evals.title: Evals compare primary and candidate routes
concepts.evals.description: Eval reports inspect metadata-only primary, candidate, and judge outcomes. Runs must be produced by existing eval configuration or ingestion; this page stays read-only.
concepts.shadowTraffic.title: Shadow Traffic mirrors without affecting the primary path
concepts.shadowTraffic.description: Shadow traffic asynchronously mirrors sampled requests to a candidate node/model and reports comparison metadata. It never switches the live route by itself.
concepts.mcpToolGateway.title: MCP Tool Gateway governs tool proxying
concepts.mcpToolGateway.description: The MCP Tool Gateway proxies configured MCP servers with Gateway API key policy, Policy Namespace allow-lists, rate limits, and metadata-only audit. It is not model routing.
concepts.budgetScopes.title: Budgets apply across explicit scopes
concepts.budgetScopes.description: Daily token and cost budgets can apply globally, by Policy Namespace, by Team, or by Gateway API Key. Narrower scopes are checked alongside global limits.
concepts.membersRoles.title: Members use fixed OSS roles
concepts.membersRoles.description: Workspace members use Admin, Operator, or Viewer. The default local Dashboard identity is bootstrapped as Admin; custom role definitions are not part of OSS v2.8 alpha.1.
concepts.providerCatalog.title: Provider Catalog visibility follows catalog readiness
concepts.providerCatalog.description: The catalog shows active, canonical provider projections by default. Legacy, transport-only, or review-required providers can exist but are hidden until explicitly shown or configured.
